title: Patania scabra
intro: Patania scabra, also known as Patanji or Patanj, is a shrub-like plant species belonging to the family Rubiaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, making it useful in treating various health conditions such as fever, cough, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Patania scabra include flavonoids, terpenoids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional medicine, Patania scabra is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into ointments and creams for topical application.
taxonomy: Patania scabra is classified as a species of moth in the family Erebidae. This moth is part of the subfamily Arctiinae and tribe Syntomini. The tribe Syntomini is a group of moths that are primarily found in the Old World tropics and subtropics. Patania scabra is native to the Indian subcontinent and Southeast Asia. Further classification and details may be specific to its genus and species.
uses: Patania scabra is known to have medicinal uses in various traditional remedies. It is used to treat fever, cough, and other respiratory issues due to its antipyretic and expectorant properties. In Ayurvedic medicine, the plant is used to treat a range of ailments, including digestive problems and skin conditions. The plant's leaves and roots are used to prepare various remedies, including teas and decoctions. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal properties and potential applications.
